title: Asahi Kasei, Mitsui Chemicals, and Mitsubishi Chemical Sign Basic Agreement to Decarbonize Ethylene Production in Western Japan
content: Asahi Kasei, Mitsui Chemicals, and Mitsubishi Chemical signed a basic agreement to establish a joint operating entity for their two ethylene manufacturing facilities in western Japan, leveraging METIâ€™s Fiscal 2025 Support Program for Energy and Manufacturing Process Conversion in Hard-to-Abate Industries to promote initiatives such as biomass feedstock in place of petroleum-derived resources. The plan targets fiscal 2030 to discontinue the Asahi Kasei Mitsubishi Chemical Ethylene Corp. facility at the Mizushima Plant and consolidate operations at Osaka Petrochemical Industries in Takaishi, with scheduled completion of structural transitions by the end of fiscal 2029 and an expected 506,000 t CO2 per year reduction in Scopes 1 and 2 compared with baseline years. An initial facility applying Asahi Kaseiâ€™s Revolefin technology will be installed at Mizushima to produce decarbonized ethylene and propylene, aiming for joint commercial production in fiscal 2034, while the vacated Mizushima site will be examined for carbon-neutral uses.
author: Analysts Account
